module-3-clean-release-snapshot.label: Project: Prepare a Clean Release Snapshot
module-3-clean-release-snapshot.summary: Prepare a volunteer toolkit for handoff by renaming, removing, ignoring, restoring, committing, and auditing one coherent release cleanup.

try_module_3_sketch5.title: Commit and Verify the Release Cleanup

try_module_3_sketch0.title: Inspect the Release Candidate
try_module_3_sketch0.prompt:
You are working in the prepared `release-kit/` repository.

Use short status and the one-line history to audit the starting point before changing any files.
try_module_3_sketch0.hint: Read the timeline from newest checkpoint to oldest.

help.concept: A safe cleanup begins by establishing the current tracked state and history.
help.hint_1: Focus on commit order and subject lines rather than machine-specific hashes.
help.hint_2: The newest entry should describe the result from this milestone.

try_module_3_sketch0.instructions:
You are working in the prepared `release-kit/` repository.

Use short status and the one-line history to audit the starting point before changing any files.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf release-snapshot
mkdir -p release-snapshot
git -C release-snapshot init -q -b main
git -C release-snapshot config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git -C release-snapshot config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
cd release-snapshot
printf %s '# Volunteer Toolkit
' > README.md
cd ..
cd release-snapshot
mkdir -p docs
printf %s '# Draft Volunteer Guide
' > docs/guide-draft.md
cd ..
cd release-snapshot
mkdir -p docs
printf %s 'Old checklist
' > docs/old-checklist.md
cd ..
git -C release-snapshot add -A
git -C release-snapshot commit -q -m 'Add project baseline'

try_module_3_sketch1.title: Rename the Volunteer Guide
try_module_3_sketch1.prompt:
You are working in the prepared `release-snapshot/` repository.

Rename `docs/guide-draft.md` to `docs/volunteer-guide.md` with Git and verify the staged structural change.
try_module_3_sketch1.hint: Move the tracked path through Git so the structural change is staged.

help.concept: The rename becomes the first staged part of the release cleanup.
help.hint_1: Confirm that the new path exists and the old path no longer appears as tracked.
help.hint_2: Review the staged name-status summary before committing.

try_module_3_sketch1.solutionCode:
git mv docs/guide-draft.md docs/volunteer-guide.md
git diff --staged --name-status
try_module_3_sketch1.instructions:
You are working in the prepared `release-snapshot/` repository.

Rename `docs/guide-draft.md` to `docs/volunteer-guide.md` with Git and verify the staged structural change.

try_module_3_sketch2.title: Remove the Obsolete Checklist
try_module_3_sketch2.prompt:
You are working in the prepared `release-snapshot/` repository.

The guide rename is already staged. Remove `docs/old-checklist.md` with Git, then review both staged changes.
try_module_3_sketch2.hint: Remove the obsolete tracked path through Git so the deletion is staged.

help.concept: The project cleanup accumulates without committing until the intended release boundary is complete.
help.hint_1: Earlier commits will still preserve the old file.
help.hint_2: Verify the deletion alongside any related rename before recording the cleanup.

try_module_3_sketch2.instructions:
You are working in the prepared `release-snapshot/` repository.

The guide rename is already staged. Remove `docs/old-checklist.md` with Git, then review both staged changes.

try_module_3_sketch3.title: Ignore Preview Output
try_module_3_sketch3.prompt:
You are working in the prepared `release-kit/` repository.

Create `.gitignore` with `*.log`, stage the policy, then use status and the staged path list to confirm that the log stays outside the release snapshot.
try_module_3_sketch3.hint: Start in the editor and make only the content change described in the task.

help.concept: The ignore policy belongs in history; the generated preview does not.
help.hint_1: Keep the file path and exact requested text in view.
help.hint_2: Switch to the terminal only after the saved file matches the scenario.

try_module_3_sketch3.solutionCode:
printf "*.log\n" > .gitignore
git add .gitignore
git status --short
git diff --staged --name-only
try_module_3_sketch3.instructions:
You are working in the prepared `release-kit/` repository.

Create `.gitignore` with `*.log`, stage the policy, then use status and the staged path list to confirm that the log stays outside the release snapshot.

try_module_3_sketch4.title: Restore an Accidental README Edit
try_module_3_sketch4.prompt:
You are working in the prepared `release-snapshot/` repository.

The cleanup is staged, but `README.md` has an accidental unstaged edit. Restore only README and verify the intended staged work remains.
try_module_3_sketch4.hint: Return the working file to the recorded version only after confirming the local edit is unwanted.

help.concept: A targeted restore removes an accidental working-tree edit without disturbing the staged cleanup.
help.hint_1: This operation targets the working tree, not existing commits.
help.hint_2: Verify that the unstaged patch disappears while unrelated staged work remains.

try_module_3_sketch4.instructions:
You are working in the prepared `release-snapshot/` repository.

The cleanup is staged, but `README.md` has an accidental unstaged edit. Restore only README and verify the intended staged work remains.

try_module_3_sketch5.title: Commit and Verify the Clean Release
try_module_3_sketch5.prompt:
You are working in the prepared `release-kit/` repository.

The rename, removal, ignore policy, and restored README are ready. Commit the cleanup as `Prepare clean volunteer release`, read the one-line history, and confirm a clean state.
try_module_3_sketch5.hint: Record only the staged snapshot and use the message requested by the scenario.

help.concept: The final verification should prove the release snapshot, history, and working tree all agree.
help.hint_1: The message should describe the project result, not the commands you used.
help.hint_2: After recording it, inspect history and any work that remains.

try_module_3_sketch5.solutionCode:
git commit -m "Prepare clean volunteer release"
git log --oneline
git status --short
try_module_3_sketch5.instructions:
You are working in the prepared `release-kit/` repository.

The rename, removal, ignore policy, and restored README are ready. Commit the cleanup as `Prepare clean volunteer release`, read the one-line history, and confirm a clean state.

moduleProject.description:
Prepare the volunteer toolkit for handoff without rewriting history. Audit the repository, stage a guide rename and obsolete-file removal, add a precise log-file ignore policy, restore an unrelated README mistake, and create one clean release checkpoint.

Finish with the intended tracked paths, ignored preview output, a readable latest commit, and no unfinished work.

intro-sketch.title: Prepare a Repository for Handoff
intro-sketch.bodyMarkdown:
A volunteer toolkit contains a draft guide name, an obsolete checklist, a generated preview, and one accidental README edit. None of those problems is individually difficult, but a safe handoff depends on solving them at the correct Git layer.

You will audit the starting state, stage a rename and removal, add an ignore policy, restore the unrelated mistake, and record one focused release snapshot. Each milestone carries the completed repository state forward.

The handoff is complete only when the history is readable, the intended paths are tracked, generated output stays ignored, and status is clean.
